title: To buy this Bay Area home, you’ll need Anthropic equity
description: Someone’s offering an unusual deal for a 13-acre property in Mill Valley, just north of South Francisco.

title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
description: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

title: India’s Snabbit seeks fresh funding at a $400M valuation, sources say
description: Snabbit has scaled rapidly, crossing one million jobs in March, amid growing investor interest.

title: Lachy Groom to back India startup Pronto at a $200M valuation, sources say
description: This round, should it occur, would double the house-help startup's valuation in a matter of weeks.

title: Cohere acquires, merges with Germany-based startup to create a ‘transatlantic AI powerhouse’
description: Cohere, the Canada-based AI company that makes AI tools for businesses in regulated industries, announced Friday it would merge with Aleph Alpha, a German company that also builds AI systems for businesses and governments.

title: Two college kids raise a $5.1 million pre-seed to build an AI social network in iMessage
description: Series, a social networking app that's grown popular on college campuses, announced a $5.1 million pre-seed round from some big names in tech.

title: Apple’s new CEO, and why Elon Musk wants to buy Cursor for $60B

title: Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga joins stacked StrictlyVC SF lineup for April 30 event
description: Surprise! StrictlyVC San Francisco, which will kick off this year’s events lineup for TechCrunch on April 30 at the Sentro Filipino Cultural Center, is getting a new addition to its increasingly stacked lineup of speakers. Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli N

title: Tim Cook is stepping down. What happens to Apple now?
description: Tim Cook plans to step down from his CEO role in September, handing the reins to hardware chief John Ternus.   Ternus may be inheriting one of the most durable businesses in tech, but he’s also stepping into a very different ecosy

title: Redwood Materials loses COO amid layoffs, restructuring
description: Former Tesla executive Chris Lister is retiring, and at least three other VPs have left Redwood around the recent restructuring, TechCrunch has learned.

title: Bret Taylor’s Sierra buys YC-backed AI startup Fragment
description: Sierra, the AI customer service agent startup founded by technologist Bret Taylor, announced today that it has acquired the YC-backed French startup Fragment.

title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
description: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

title: Don’t stop hiring humans — stop hiring the wrong humans, Artisan’s founder says
description: Artisan may be known for their bold “Stop Hiring Humans” campaign but the reality is every founder needs to assemble the right team if they want to scale.

title: Era raises $11M to build a software platform for AI gadgets
description: Era thinks that we will see many form factors of AI hardware, including glasses, rings, and pendants.

title: Grab a ticket today: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 kicks off in just a week in San Francisco
description: StrictlyVC San Francisco is in just a week. Now’s the time to grab yourself a ticket. Join VCs and founders at Sentro Filipino Cultural Center on April 30.

title: Another customer of troubled startup Delve suffered a big security incident
description: TechCrunch has confirmed that Delve was the compliance company that performed the security certifications for Context AI, the AI agent training startup that last week disclosed a security incident.

title: Beehiiv rolls out new creator tools, including webinars and customizable paywalls
description: The announcement is a clear sign the company is trying to become an all-in-one hub for creators, reducing the hassle of juggling various tools and services to run their businesses.

title: Fusion doesn’t have a normal startup timeline, and investors are fine with that
